no_valid_candidate_pool_failure.interpretation: If the planned candidate pool has no valid goal candidate, any terminal commit is invalid regardless of local-context evidence.

wrong_instance_arbitration_failure.interpretation: Own-view detector/SAM2 category evidence can support a wrong repeated instance more strongly than the correct goal instance.

design_principle.fact: Local-context detector/SAM2 substrate is available, but the first terminal local-context rule commits wrong and no-valid goals.
design_principle.agent_inference: The revised objective must treat own-view category evidence as object visibility evidence, not as ObjectNav goal validity evidence.
design_principle.novelty_constraint: The contribution remains semantic uncertainty as active SLAM/navigation utility only if uncertainty routes to pool repair or goal-validity confirmation instead of becoming detector-score re-ranking.

allowed_action_inputs: candidate ids, semantic ranks, and non-GT scores
candidate roles from the frozen local-context plan
detector box, mask, and candidate-association evidence
non-GT spatial consistency between source, detector, and local-context candidates
Habitat navmesh reachability and standoff metadata

route_priority.condition: pool_validity_guard_v2 cannot establish an action-time valid candidate pool
pool_validity_guard_v2 is unresolved rather than failed
candidate pool appears valid but repeated-instance goal validity is not uniquely established
multiple candidates remain compatible with goal validity after local-context evidence
one candidate passes pool-validity and wrong-instance arbitration guards using action-time evidence

terminal_commit_requirements.name: pool_validity_guard_passed
terminal_commit_requirements.rule: A terminal commit is forbidden when the action-time evidence only says that an object/category is visible but cannot support that at least one candidate in the pool is a plausible ObjectNav goal.

terminal_commit_requirements.name: unique_goal_valid_candidate
terminal_commit_requirements.rule: The committed candidate must be uniquely supported as a goal-valid instance, not merely as the strongest own-view detection.

terminal_commit_requirements.name: wrong_instance_arbitration_resolved
terminal_commit_requirements.rule: If a wrong detector-strong candidate and a weak correct-compatible candidate remain plausible, the policy must request confirmation or defer.

terminal_commit_requirements.name: no_detector_score_shortcut
terminal_commit_requirements.rule: Detector score, source rank, own-view support, and local-context-only support cannot independently trigger a terminal commit.

terminal_commit_requirements.name: evaluation_only_label_separation
terminal_commit_requirements.rule: Candidate correctness, wrong-goal labels, and no-valid labels may be joined only after action rows are written.

interpretation_rules.safe_but_inert: If the revised rule produces zero wrong/no-valid commits but no safe success commits, it is a safety result, not a utility proof.
interpretation_rules.unsafe_nonzero: If the revised rule produces any wrong/no-valid commit, terminal utility remains blocked and failure diagnosis is required before revision.
interpretation_rules.safe_nonzero: If the revised rule produces zero wrong/no-valid commits and nonzero success commits while outperforming defer_all, it can become a paper-facing candidate for fresh validation.
interpretation_rules.no_threshold_repair: Changing numeric thresholds after seeing joined labels is not a valid repair.

next_steps: Implement revised local-context analyzer from this contract.
Run Docker py_compile or equivalent smoke check.
Run paper-scale local-context revision evaluation on the frozen rows.
Join labels only after decision rows are written.
Update failure taxonomy before any policy-scale comparison.
